objective: Resolve only HCM-R2-F-001 by enforcing exact historical v1.0 ID and SHA-256 byte-fingerprint admission, add deterministic negative proof, align only the coupled protocol/proof wording, and leave HCM-0.1 for a different fresh reviewer.
work_completed: Used the dispatch-recorded LOW GitNexus upstream impact for validator main: one direct file-level caller and zero affected processes/modules; the indexed target had not changed before the edit.
Added an immutable admission mapping for exactly the seven historical v1.0 canonical filenames, matching handoff IDs, and SHA-256 fingerprints of exact record bytes.

Made v1.0 validation fail closed for unknown filenames/IDs, filename/handoff-ID mismatches, and byte-modified admitted records while retaining ordinary schema routing for admitted v1.0 and all v1.1 records.

Added a deterministic bounded temp-copy self-test that proves an otherwise-schema-valid new v1.0 record fails after an exact ledger rebuild and that a byte-modified admitted v1.0 record fails for its SHA-256 mismatch.
Aligned only the HCM-0.1 proof gate and handoff routing/closeout/failure wording; no existing record, dispatch, schema, template, Rust file, test, core asset, or skill changed in this remediation.

deliverables.summary: Exact historical-v1.0 filename/ID/SHA-256 admission enforcement plus deterministic negative self-test.
Protocol, closeout, and failure rules aligned to the fail-closed historical-v1.0 admission boundary.
HCM-0.1 proof gate now requires deterministic rejection of unknown and byte-modified v1.0 records.
Immutable v1.1 remediation handoff leaving HCM-0.1 review-required.

decisions.summary: Historical v1.0 compatibility is admitted only by the validator-owned exact seven-entry canonical filename, handoff ID, and SHA-256 byte-fingerprint mapping; date or schema validity alone never admits v1.0.
decisions.authority_effect: Mechanically enforces the existing mandatory-v1.1 new-record rule without rewriting historical records or changing either schema/template.

decisions.summary: HCM-R2-F-001 is resolved by normal validation plus deterministic unknown/modified-v1.0 negative proof, but this remediation session does not review or close HCM-0.1.
decisions.authority_effect: HCM-0.1 remains review_required until orchestration dispatches another different fresh independent reviewer and that review returns clean.

validation.evidence_ref: LOW risk; one direct file-level caller; zero affected processes/modules.
Two record schemas, current v1.1 template, all 12 canonical records, all 12 ledger entries, exact parity, and byte-identical deterministic rebuild passed.
Otherwise-schema-valid unknown v1.0 record with exact rebuilt ledger was rejected; byte-modified admitted v1.0 record was rejected for SHA-256 mismatch.

validation.command: Changed-doc relative-link, portability, and active archive-boundary assertions for 06 and 08
validation.evidence_ref: All relative targets resolve; no changed active doc links into archives; no machine-absolute path appears.

validation.command: git status --porcelain=v1 --untracked-files=all docs-only assertion and git diff --check
validation.evidence_ref: All dirty paths remain under docs/specs/handbook-contract-membrane/, and the working-tree whitespace check passes.
Eight indexed changed files, 30 symbols, zero affected processes, LOW risk; no commit requested.

sections: Historical v1.0 admission mapping and negative self-test
pack_updates.reason: Resolve HCM-R2-F-001 by rejecting any new or byte-modified v1.0 record.
Align protocol wording to the exact ID/content-fingerprint admission boundary and negative proof command.
Require refusal of unknown or byte-modified historical-v1.0 records before HCM-0.1 closeout.

next_session.objective: Select this remediation handoff and dispatch another different fresh independent reviewer to re-review the complete HCM-0.1 control pack, including HCM-R2-F-001 admission enforcement; close HCM-0.1 only if that reviewer reports no actionable finding.
allowed_scope: Orchestrate one different fresh, docs-only independent review of the complete HCM-0.1 control pack and handoff validation infrastructure.
Require the normal validator and historical-v1.0 negative self-test against live repository truth.
prohibited_scope: Do not use this remediation agent or the prior fresh reviewer as the next independent reviewer.
Do not close HCM-0.1 from this remediation handoff alone.
Do not rewrite immutable records or dispatches, change schemas/templates, implement Rust, modify tests/core assets/skills, select defaults, or start later slices.
acceptance_criteria: A different fresh independent reviewer confirms all seven historical v1.0 records remain valid and any unknown or byte-modified v1.0 record fails deterministically.
The normal full validator and negative self-test remain green after the review handoff and ledger append.
The reviewer reports no unresolved actionable finding before HCM-0.1 closes.
stop_conditions: Keep HCM-0.1 open if any actionable finding remains.
Dispatch a separate bounded remediation instead of widening the review if a new issue appears.

chat_summary: Resolved HCM-R2-F-001 with exact seven-record historical v1.0 filename/ID/SHA-256 admission and deterministic unknown/modified-v1.0 negative proof. HCM-0.1 remains review-required for another different fresh reviewer; no immutable history, schema/template, Rust, tests, core assets, or skills changed.
